name: Phosgène
description: 2HP digital oscillator pairing an 11-bit wavefolded wavetable engine (2 banks of 30 waves) with a 100Hz-base FM engine. Coarse/Fold/Mod knobs feed separate Wave Out and FM Out. Tracks 1V/Oct over 8 octaves with octave and octave-displace (wavetables only) switches.

controls.name: Coarse
controls.description: Main frequency control for both engines. Sets wavetable pitch and the FM carrier (sine) frequency.

controls.name: Fold
controls.description: Wavefolding amount applied to the selected wavetable. Drives the Wave Out from mellow shapes into industrial digital textures.

controls.name: Mod
controls.description: Modulation depth for the FM engine. Pushes/pulls the amplitude of the modulator against the 100Hz base to reach the FM Out timbre.

controls.name: Wave Select (button)
controls.description: Short press steps to the next wavetable in the current bank. Long press switches bank (Bank 1 / Bank 2).

controls.name: Octave (button)
controls.description: Short press steps the oscillator octave up (cycling). Long press steps down. Three octave positions around the tracked range.

controls.name: Octave Displace (switch)
controls.description: Shifts wavetable playback about one octave further down the scale. FM path keeps its full range.

inputs.name: Fold CV
inputs.description: CV over the Fold knob. Modulates wavefolding amount on the wavetable path.

inputs.name: Mod CV
inputs.description: CV over the Mod knob. Modulates FM depth on the FM path.

outputs.name: Wave Out
outputs.description: Folded wavetable output. Fed by Coarse pitch, Fold amount and the selected bank/wave.

outputs.name: FM Out
outputs.description: FM engine output. Sine carrier modulated against a 100Hz base, scaled by Mod.

behaviors.name: Parallel wavetable and FM paths
behaviors.description: The wavetable/fold engine and the FM engine share Coarse and pitch but run in parallel to their own jacks. One knob set drives two different voices, so Phosgène often replaces two oscillators in a small case.

behaviors.name: 100Hz FM base
behaviors.description: FM is built around a 100Hz carrier base instead of tracking a normal audio-rate sine. The result is a darker, more sober spectrum that sits well for tight FM basses and deep, controlled modulations rather than bright classic FM bells.

behaviors.name: Wavefolding as the main timbre control
behaviors.description: Fold is the primary voicing control for Wave Out. Low Fold gives clean wavetable tones; higher Fold pushes into the brand's 'industrial poison' character, and Fold CV lets an envelope or LFO open the voice dynamically.

behaviors.name: Octave displace (wavetables only)
behaviors.description: The displace switch drops only the wavetable path about an octave further down. FM Out stays where it is, so the same V/Oct can drive a sub-like folded layer while FM holds a mid-range voice.

behaviors.name: Stored startup state
behaviors.description: The module recalls the last bank, wave and octave settings after reboot. It boots ready-to-play, which matters in a 2HP tile where you cannot see a preset page.

behaviors.name: 11-bit aliasing at the top end
behaviors.description: The 11-bit wavetable engine plus high octave positions deliberately produces digital trash and aliasing. Useful for brittle leads, metallic percussion and noisy texture rather than polite sine-like tones.

patchIdeas.name: Parallel voice split (one module, two voices)
patchIdeas.patch: Treat Wave Out and FM Out as two oscillators sharing pitch. Send Wave Out into a filter for a classic folded lead and FM Out into a distortion or another filter for a darker parallel voice, then mix them. One V/Oct drives both, so melodies stay locked.
patchIdeas.walkthrough: 1. Patch a sequencer or keyboard to Phosgène V/Oct. 2. Set Coarse to a comfortable pitch, Fold around 10 o'clock, Mod around 12 o'clock, Octave Displace off. 3. Wave Out -> Filter In -> Mixer A. 4. FM Out -> Distortion In -> Mixer B. 5. Play the sequence and balance the two channels on the mixer: Fold shapes the bright layer, Mod shapes the dark layer.
patchIdeas.hear: A single note becomes a stacked voice: a folded wavetable lead riding on top of a darker FM body, both perfectly in tune because they share Coarse and V/Oct.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Phosgène:
* Coarse: mid
* Fold: ~10 o'clock
* Mod: ~12 o'clock
* Wave Select: Bank 1, mid wave
* Octave: 0
* Octave Displace: off

Sequencer:
Filter:
Distortion:
Mixer:

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Phosgène (V/Oct)
- Phosgène (Wave Out) -> Filter (In)
- Phosgène (FM Out) -> Distortion (In)
- Filter (Out) -> Mixer (In 1)
- Distortion (Out) -> Mixer (In 2)

patchIdeas.name: Dark FM bass from FM Out
patchIdeas.patch: Lean on the 100Hz FM base for a tight bass. An envelope drives Mod CV so the FM depth opens on each note, while Wave Out is ignored (or used later as a harmonic layer).
patchIdeas.walkthrough: 1. Sequencer -> Phosgène V/Oct, low octave. 2. Coarse sets bass pitch; start Mod at ~9 o'clock. 3. Envelope (short, punchy) -> Mod CV so each gate pushes FM depth up. 4. FM Out -> VCA In. 5. Same envelope (or a copy) -> VCA CV so the note is also amplitude-shaped. 6. VCA Out -> output. Tune Mod knob and envelope amount until the attack has bite but the sustain stays clean.
patchIdeas.hear: A tight, slightly metallic bass with a dark attack transient from the FM sweep. The 100Hz base keeps it focused instead of going into classic bright FM territory.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Phosgène:
* Coarse: low
* Fold: unused
* Mod: ~9 o'clock (env will add more)
* Octave: -1
* Octave Displace: off

Sequencer:
Envelope:
VCA:
Output:

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Phosgène (V/Oct)
- Sequencer (Gate) g> Envelope (Trig)
- Envelope (Out) >> Phosgène (Mod CV)
- Phosgène (FM Out) -> VCA (In)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCA (CV)
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Drone with displaced sub and folded top
patchIdeas.patch: Use Octave Displace so Wave Out sits an octave lower than FM Out. A slow LFO into Fold CV moves the wavetable texture while FM Out holds a mid drone. Both paths mix into a filter.
patchIdeas.walkthrough: 1. No sequencer: just tune Coarse to a drone pitch. 2. Flip Octave Displace ON - Wave Out drops about an octave, FM Out stays. 3. Slow LFO (~20s cycle) -> Fold CV so the wavetable shape breathes. 4. Long-press Wave Select to pick Bank 2, then short-press to land on a wave with motion. 5. Wave Out -> Mixer In 1, FM Out -> Mixer In 2. 6. Mixer Out -> Filter In -> Output. Open the filter until the folded highs appear, then back off.
patchIdeas.hear: A sustained drone with a low folded sub layer slowly morphing under a stable FM mid. Moves on its own for minutes without touching anything.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Phosgène:
* Coarse: drone pitch
* Fold: ~11 o'clock (LFO will move it)
* Mod: ~10 o'clock
* Wave Select: Bank 2, motion wave
* Octave: 0
* Octave Displace: on

LFO (slow):
Mixer:
Filter:
Output:

- LFO (Out) >> Phosgène (Fold CV)
- Phosgène (Wave Out) -> Mixer (In 1)
- Phosgène (FM Out) -> Mixer (In 2)
- Mixer (Out) -> Filter (In)
- Filter (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Glitch percussion from Wave Out
patchIdeas.patch: Push the octave high and slam Fold CV with a fast envelope. Wave Out becomes a brittle digital percussion voice; FM Out can run alongside as a metallic accent.
patchIdeas.walkthrough: 1. Clock -> trigger source for a short, snappy envelope. 2. Envelope -> Fold CV (large amount) so each trigger snaps the folder wide open. 3. Tap the Octave button so Phosgène sits in its highest position; pick a noisy wave. 4. Wave Out -> VCA In. 5. Same envelope -> VCA CV so you get a short percussive gate. 6. VCA Out -> output. For a second layer, send FM Out -> separate VCA with a slightly different envelope for a metallic ping.
patchIdeas.hear: Short, bright, broken-glass hits at the top end of the module's digital range - usable as hi-hats, glitch blips or metallic percussion in a tight 2HP footprint.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Phosgène:
* Coarse: mid
* Fold: low (env will open it)
* Mod: ~10 o'clock
* Wave Select: Bank 1, noisy wave
* Octave: high
* Octave Displace: off

Clock:
Envelope:
VCA:
Output:

- Clock (Out) t> Envelope (Trig)
- Envelope (Out) >> Phosgène (Fold CV)
- Phosgène (Wave Out) -> VCA (In)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCA (CV)
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In)
